title: The 24-Hour Rule & Delayed Gratification
learningObjectives: Practice the 24-hour rule before non-essential purchases
Understand delayed gratification as a superpower
Recognize that patience is a learnable financial skill
keyPrinciples: Waiting 24 hours on impulse purchases eliminates ~70% of them
Urgency in ads is almost always manufactured
storyPrompts: {name} adds the {item} to their cart. Finger hovering over checkout. Then they remember the rule: close the tab, come back tomorrow. Twenty-four hours later, they open their browser — and realize they don't want it anymore. The $45 stays in savings.
The marshmallow experiment: kids who could wait got better life outcomes. {name} thinks about this while staring at {item} in the store window. 'Am I the marshmallow kid... or the wait-for-two kid?'

A mentor tells {name}: 'Every major purchase I've regretted, I made fast. Every purchase I'm glad I made, I thought about for at least a day.' {name} puts the rule on their phone lock screen: WAIT 24 HOURS.

decisionCards.scenario: The website says 'Only 2 left in stock!' for the {item} you've been eyeing.
decisionCards.context: Scarcity tactics are one of the oldest sales tricks in the book.

choices.label: Buy now before it's gone!
choices.description: Scarcity is real sometimes... right?
choices.opportunityCost: Retailers restock constantly. This urgency was manufactured.

choices.label: Apply the 24-hour rule
choices.description: If it's meant to be yours, it'll be there.

choices.label: Search for the same thing elsewhere
choices.description: Competition means it's probably not the last one.

decisionCards.scenario: You've waited 24 hours. You still want the {item}.
decisionCards.context: The rule worked — this desire is real. Now what?

choices.label: Buy it — you passed the test!
choices.description: You've confirmed it's not an impulse.

choices.label: Wait another week and save for it
choices.description: Even real desires deserve planning.

choices.label: Find a way to earn it rather than spend for it
choices.description: Make the money before you spend it.

decisionCards.scenario: Your friend bought the thing you've been waiting on. They love it.

choices.label: Fine, I'll get it too
choices.description: Their happiness is contagious!

choices.label: Ask to borrow it first
choices.description: Try before buy — smart move.

choices.label: Stick to your own timeline
choices.description: Their purchase doesn't change your financial plan.

decisionCards.scenario: You've been putting off buying something you ACTUALLY need.
decisionCards.context: The 24-hour rule is for wants. Don't delay real needs.

choices.label: Keep waiting — the rule says wait!
choices.description: Rules are rules...

choices.label: Buy it — it's a genuine need
choices.description: Know the difference between wants and needs.

choices.label: Find the most cost-effective option first
choices.description: Need it, but find the best price.

decisionCards.scenario: A flash sale ends in 2 hours. The {item} is 60% off.
decisionCards.context: This is the hardest test of the 24-hour rule.

choices.label: Buy immediately — 60% off is real!
choices.description: This IS a legitimately great deal.

choices.label: Ask: would I want this at full price?
choices.description: If not, the sale is still a waste.

choices.label: Skip it — I didn't plan for it
choices.description: Unplanned is unplanned, even at a discount.

decisionCards.scenario: You've been craving a treat all day. It's 11pm and the food app is open.
decisionCards.context: Late-night spending is the graveyard of good financial intentions.

choices.label: Order it — YOLO
choices.description: You deserve it after a long day.

choices.label: Drink water and go to sleep
choices.description: Most late-night cravings are gone by morning.

choices.label: Plan a real treat for tomorrow
choices.description: Turn impulse into intention.

decisionCards.scenario: Your impulse buy from last week arrived. You feel... meh.
decisionCards.context: The let-down after an impulse buy is real. This is data.

choices.label: Justify the purchase to yourself
choices.description: 'I must need it more than I think.'

choices.label: Return it if possible
choices.description: Own the mistake, recover the money.

choices.label: Use the feeling as a reminder to wait next time
choices.description: Data > regret.

decisionCards.scenario: Your entire friend group seems to have more stuff than you. It stings.
decisionCards.context: Comparison is the thief of financial peace.

choices.label: Start spending more to keep up
choices.description: You deserve nice things too!

choices.label: Remember your goals and your plan
choices.description: Your path is different from theirs.

choices.label: Realize they might be in debt
choices.description: Visible spending doesn't mean they're winning.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: End of week — you successfully applied the 24-hour rule 3 times.
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Each wait was a victory. Now you have $90 extra. What do you do with it?

choices.label: Spend it — you earned the right to splurge
choices.description: The waiting should have some reward!

choices.label: Invest the full $90
choices.description: Patience compounding into more patience.

choices.label: Bucket split it
choices.description: Reward + future + growth.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: You've been waiting 6 months to buy something. You finally have the money.

choices.label: Buy it immediately — you waited!
choices.description: Six months of patience earns this.

choices.label: Ask if you still want it as much
choices.description: Sometimes waiting reveals it was never worth it.

choices.label: Buy a smaller version and invest the rest
choices.description: Capture the win AND keep growing.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: Someone offers to buy you something right now — no strings. Do you take it?

choices.label: Yes! Free is free!
choices.description: Who turns down free stuff?

choices.label: Ask why they're offering
choices.description: Free gifts can create social debt.

choices.label: Decline politely
choices.description: Your independence is worth more than a gift.

forecasts: Your patience trait is surging. Expect high-reward, high-wait opportunities next week!
You rushed a few purchases. Temptation traps increase next week. Slow down.
Perfect 24-hour rule week! Bonus savings event triggered for next session.
Your discipline is consistent. Investment growth rate increases slightly next week.
